summary.scholar: Fought July 1-4, 1863, Gettysburg was the bloodiest battle of the Civil War with 51,116 total casualties. Lees invasion of the North was repulsed.
summary.explorer: For three days in July 1863, the biggest battle of the Civil War was fought in Gettysburg, Pennsylvania. Over 51,000 soldiers were killed or wounded.

significance.scholar: Gettysburg is widely regarded as the turning point of the Civil War.
significance.explorer: This was the turning point of the war. After Gettysburg, the Union started winning more.
